description: Restyle inline code elements to be less visually heavy while maintaining the neo-brutalist retro aesthetic. Currently, inline code uses the same dark Dracula background as code blocks, creating dense black areas when multiple inline code spans appear in a paragraph.
qualityChecks: cd docs && npm run build && npm run type-check && npm run lint && npm run knip && npm run format:check

userStories.title: Add inline-code-specific CSS custom properties and restyle inline code elements
userStories.description: As a docs reader, I want inline code to be visually distinct but lighter than code blocks so that paragraphs with many inline code spans are readable without dense black areas.
acceptanceCriteria: New CSS custom properties --inline-code-bg and --inline-code-fg are defined in both :root (light) and .dark (dark) in docs/app/global.css
Light mode --inline-code-bg is a subtle warm gray (not the dark Dracula #282a36), e.g. #f0ede6 or similar muted warm tone
Dark mode --inline-code-bg is a subtle dark surface (lighter than code blocks), e.g. #2a2a2a or similar
Light mode --inline-code-fg uses a dark foreground for contrast against the light background, e.g. #4a3f2f or similar warm dark tone
Dark mode --inline-code-fg uses a light but not white foreground, e.g. #e0d8c8 or similar warm light tone
Tailwind theme tokens --color-inline-code-bg and --color-inline-code-fg are registered in the @theme inline block in docs/app/global.css
The inline code component in docs/components/MDX.tsx uses bg-inline-code-bg and text-inline-code-fg instead of bg-code-bg and text-primary
A thin border is added to inline code using border and border-border/30 (or similar low-opacity border) to give it definition without heaviness, fitting the neo-brutalist sharp-corner aesthetic
Inline code retains font-mono, text-sm, and sharp corners (rounded-(--radius) with --radius: 0)
The padding is adjusted from p-1 to px-1.5 py-0.5 for a tighter inline fit that doesn't disrupt line height
Code blocks (pre > code in CodeBlock.tsx) are NOT affected — they retain bg-code-bg and text-code-fg
The overall effect is that inline code feels like a gentle highlight rather than a heavy dark block, while still looking retro and intentional
cd docs && npm run build passes
cd docs && npm run type-check passes
cd docs && npm run lint passes
cd docs && npm run knip passes
cd docs && npm run format:check passes
